pr.title: Use fuzzy code % in Wiki TU page
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: PR 2654 updated the Wiki translation-unit reporting script to display and classify code progress using fuzzy code metrics instead of strictly matched code metrics. It also made byte-size formatting replace both singular and plural byte words with B via a compiled regex.

key_files.role: Only changed file; updates Wiki TU percentage, remaining-code, row classification, and byte formatting logic.

what_changed.text: Added a module-level BYTES_RE regex and changed friendly_size to replace singular or plural byte words with B before converting spaces to non-breaking spaces.
Added get_measure_float alongside get_measure_int so percent values such as fuzzy_match_percent and matched_data_percent are read as floats with a default of 0.
Changed code percentage cells from matched_code/matched_code_percent to fuzzy_code/fuzzy_match_percent, while data percentage cells continue to use matched_data/total_data/matched_data_percent.
Changed the full row's displayed matched code size to use fuzzy_code instead of matched_code.
During unit processing, derives fuzzy_code as int(total_code * fuzzy_match_percent / 100), stores it in unit['measures'], and computes remaining_code from total_code minus fuzzy_code.
Changed classification of non-complete units with all code done from matched_code_percent >= 100 to fuzzy_match_percent >= 100 before placing them in data_left.

smart_moves.text: Centralized byte-word replacement in a compiled regex so both 'byte' and 'bytes' outputs from humanfriendly.format_size can be normalized consistently.
Introduced a typed float accessor for measure percentages rather than repeating inline float(unit['measures'].get(...) or 0) conversions.
Computed and stored fuzzy_code once during row preparation, allowing downstream display and remaining-code logic to use the same derived value.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For progress-report tooling, distinguish strict matched-code metrics from fuzzy-code metrics and use the metric that matches the page's intended semantics.
evidence_refs: pr_title:Use fuzzy code % in Wiki TU page

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a report uses a derived percentage as the authoritative measure, compute related byte counts and remaining work from that same percentage to keep table display and classification aligned.

naming_conventions.name: fuzzy_percent
naming_conventions.context: Local variable holding fuzzy_match_percent as a float during unit classification.

naming_conventions.name: fuzzy_code
naming_conventions.context: Derived measure stored in unit['measures'] for fuzzy matched code bytes.

naming_conventions.name: BYTES_RE
naming_conventions.context: Module-level compiled regex constant used by friendly_size for byte-word normalization.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Wiki TU code progress can use fuzzy code metrics
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2654 changed tools/wiki_tu.py so the Wiki TU page's code progress display and completion classification use fuzzy_code/fuzzy_match_percent instead of matched_code/matched_code_percent.
evidence_refs: pr_title:Use fuzzy code % in Wiki TU page

source_update_candidates.title: Document Wiki TU fuzzy-code metric semantics
source_update_candidates.text: If tooling documentation exists for the Wiki TU page, consider documenting that code percentage and remaining-code display are based on fuzzy_match_percent/fuzzy_code, while data percentage remains based on matched_data_percent.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR changes user-visible progress semantics but the loaded slice contains no documentation update.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that fuzzy metrics are globally preferred for all reports; the evidence is limited to tools/wiki_tu.py and the Wiki TU page.
Do not infer author or reviewer rationale beyond the PR title and diff; the PR body is empty and no human review comments are loaded.
Do not treat the GALE01 'No changes' bot report as proof that the wiki_tu script was functionally tested.
